title: arundarbor cornuta
intro: Arundarbor cornuta, also known as Deer's Horn or Antelope Horn, is a plant species native to North America, belonging to the genus Cornus. Traditionally, Arundarbor cornuta has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, making it effective in treating pain, fever, and inflammation. The plant's berries, leaves, and bark have been used in herbal preparations to treat various health issues, including rheumatism, arthritis, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Arundarbor cornuta is often combined with other herbs to create teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
